qa_pairs.question: How can one view all the command-line options the Java Virtual Machine (JVM) will use when running?
qa_pairs.answer: You can see all the command-line options the JVM is going to run with by specifying the flag `-XX:+PrintCommandLineFlags`. For example: `java -Xms256m -Xmx4g -XX:+PrintCommandLineFlags -version`.

qa_pairs.question: Is there an alternative method to set JVM run-time options instead of passing them directly on the command line?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, you can use the `JAVA_TOOL_OPTIONS` environment variable to set the run-time options.

qa_pairs.question: When is using the `JAVA_TOOL_OPTIONS` environment variable especially convenient?
qa_pairs.answer: It is especially convenient if you launch multiple Java calls, or if you call a Java program from another Java program.

qa_pairs.question: Could you provide an example of how to set the `JAVA_TOOL_OPTIONS` environment variable?
qa_pairs.answer: You can set it using a command like: `export JAVA_TOOL_OPTIONS="-Xms256m -Xmx2g"`.
